_metadata.methodology: Informed estimates based on FCO consular assistance data (~6,000 British deaths abroad annually, with Spain accounting for ~25%), UK tourist volume by destination (ONS overseas travel data), relative search demand logic (more deaths and tourist volume = more searches), general SERP knowledge, and competitor analysis (Rowland Brothers International dominates with thin content, GOV.UK dominates informational queries). No access to Ahrefs, SEMrush, or Google Keyword Planner. All volumes are estimated ranges for the UK search market.
_metadata.confidence_note: Volumes are marked as ranges with confidence levels. HIGH confidence = based on direct FCO death statistics extrapolation. MEDIUM confidence = reasonable estimate from tourist volume and relative demand. LOW confidence = speculative for niche or long-tail queries. Actual volumes should be validated with keyword tools before making content prioritisation changes.
_metadata.volume_basis: UK search market (primary). US and Australian searches would add 30-60% on top for countries relevant to those markets (USA, Thailand, Philippines, Mexico, etc.).
_metadata.competition_note: Competition is assessed against organic SERP difficulty. 'low' = fewer than 3 optimised pages targeting the exact query, thin content from incumbents. 'low-medium' = GOV.UK or Wikipedia present but beatable with depth. 'medium' = established pages with decent content. Rowland Brothers has 150+ country pages but all are extremely thin (30-50 words unique content). GOV.UK has one generic death abroad page that ranks for many informational queries.
sources_referenced: ONS Travel Trends (UK residents overseas visits by destination)
GOV.UK: When someone dies abroad guidance page
SERP analysis: manual review of Google UK results for sample queries

query_patterns.notes: Primary commercial query. Low competition because Rowland Brothers country pages are thin (30-50 words). Most results are generic funeral director pages or GOV.UK.
Crisis query. Searcher has an immediate need. Higher conversion potential than pattern 1. Often typed by someone in the first 24 hours after a death abroad.
Formal phrasing. Used by people who have already spoken to an embassy or funeral director and are now researching providers. Slightly lower volume than patterns 1-2 but very high intent.
Informational query. GOV.UK ranks here but their page is generic (not country-specific). A country-specific guide outperforms. This is the top-of-funnel entry point that bridges to commercial pages.
Price-intent query. Rowland Brothers shows 'starting from' prices but no breakdown. A detailed cost guidance section wins. High commercial value because the searcher is comparing providers.

query_patterns.pattern: someone died in [country] what to do
query_patterns.notes: Crisis informational query. Often the very first search someone makes. GOV.UK captures some of this traffic. Our country-specific guides should target this alongside pattern 4. Extremely high emotional state. Content must be sensitive.
Broad commercial query. May trigger local pack results for in-country funeral homes. Lower search volume than repatriation-specific terms but catches a different segment of searchers.

Embassy/consular query. GOV.UK dominates here and rightly so. We should include embassy contact details in our guide pages but we will not outrank GOV.UK for this pattern. Secondary targeting only. Still valuable for on-page comprehensiveness.

Ashes transport query. Completely separate silo from body repatriation. Rowland Brothers has zero ashes-specific content. No competitor targets this. Lower volume per country but virtually zero competition.
Informational query that bridges to the ashes transport service. People whose relative was cremated abroad and now need to bring ashes home. Different process, different regulations, different page.

spain.name: Spain
spain.death_volume_note: Approximately 25% of all British deaths abroad (~1,500-2,200 per year per FCO consular data). Largest British expat population in Europe (~300,000 registered, estimated 750,000+ including unregistered). Popular retirement destination. Costa del Sol, Costa Blanca, Canary Islands, Balearics are hotspots.
spain.tourist_volume_note: 18-20 million UK visits per year (ONS). Most visited country by British tourists.

ship ashes from spain

france.name: France
france.death_volume_note: Second highest British deaths abroad (~500-700 per year). Large expat community in southern France. Proximity means many deaths are elderly residents, not tourists.
france.tourist_volume_note: 12-14 million UK visits per year. Second most visited destination. Short-haul, frequent travel.

thailand.name: Thailand
thailand.death_volume_note: Approximately 300-400 British deaths per year. Disproportionately high search volume relative to deaths because Thailand deaths are often sudden (accidents, drowning, violence) which creates more acute crisis searches. Long-stay tourists and retirees in Pattaya, Chiang Mai, Phuket.
thailand.tourist_volume_note: 1-1.5 million UK visits per year. Long-haul but extremely popular. High media coverage of deaths abroad in Thailand amplifies search demand.

keywords.keyword: funeral repatriation from thailand
bring body home from thailand
repatriation of remains from thailand
death in thailand what to do
cost to repatriate body from thailand
someone died in thailand what to do
british embassy thailand death
ship ashes from thailand
cremation in thailand bring ashes home
keywords.notes: Thailand has strong Buddhist cremation culture. Many families opt for cremation in Thailand rather than body repatriation. This query captures that segment.

usa.name: USA

usa.tourist_volume_note: 4-5 million UK visits per year. Third most popular long-haul destination. Business and leisure travel.

generic_keywords.notes: Head term. Highest volume. Homepage and pillar page target.

generic_keywords.keyword: repatriation of a body to the uk

generic_keywords.notes: GOV.UK dominates but their page is generic. Country-specific guides and a strong overview page can capture PAA and additional SERP real estate.

generic_keywords.keyword: what happens when someone dies abroad

generic_keywords.notes: Zinc lining is a legal requirement for many countries. Families search this when they learn about requirements.
High volume but competitive with insurance comparison sites. Informational guide about what travel insurance covers for repatriation. Affiliate potential.
Navigational intent toward GOV.UK. Low conversion potential but captures visitors who can't find what they need on GOV.UK.

generic_keywords.keyword: can you fly with ashes internationally

generic_keywords.notes: Some families try to post ashes via Royal Mail. Royal Mail does accept ashes. This captures that segment.

generic_keywords.keyword: what to do when someone dies abroad uk

total_estimated_monthly_searches.notes: These are UK-only estimates. Adding US, Australian, and other English-speaking markets would increase total addressable by 30-60%, particularly for USA, Australia, Thailand, and Philippines country clusters.

phase_1.rationale: These 10+ pages capture ~60% of total addressable search volume. Spain alone accounts for ~10%.

phase_2.rationale: Complete the P1 country coverage. Insurance guide captures high-volume informational traffic.

phase_3.rationale: Long-tail coverage. Low individual volume but virtually zero competition. Each page should rank within weeks.

highest_value_keywords.reason: Head term, homepage target, highest raw volume
Informational gateway. Captures families at moment of crisis. Bridges to service pages.
Price intent = high conversion. Dedicated costs page can rank and convert.

highest_value_keywords.keyword: what happens when someone dies abroad
highest_value_keywords.reason: Alternative phrasing of the crisis query. Same page target as death abroad.
High volume, moderate competition. Insurance guide with affiliate potential.

highest_value_keywords.keyword: what to do when someone dies abroad uk
highest_value_keywords.reason: UK-qualified informational query. GOV.UK competition but huge PAA opportunity.
Highest volume country-specific query. Spain = 25% of deaths abroad.

zero_competition_opportunities: Ship ashes from [any country] - nobody targets this
Cremation in [country] bring ashes home - nobody targets this
Country-specific death abroad guides - GOV.UK is generic only
Cost to repatriate from [specific country] - no detailed cost guidance exists
Reverse direction repatriation (UK to India/Philippines/Kenya/Morocco) - diaspora market completely unserved
Zinc lined coffin requirements by country - no resource exists
Airline ashes transport rules - no comprehensive guide exists

reverse_repatriation_opportunity.description: Sending remains FROM the UK TO the family's country of origin. Diaspora communities in the UK searching for this service.
reverse_repatriation_opportunity.notes: This is a completely unserved market in SEO. Rowland Brothers does offer this service but has zero targeted content. A dedicated section or page per diaspora community could capture this traffic with zero competition.
